title: GOGIS present alternative plots to owners of illegally obtained lands In Gombe
description: The Gombe Geographic Information System (GOGIS) has commenced the allocation of alternative plots to individuals who unknowingly acquired lands illegally allocated on designated access roads in parts of Gombe metropolis. The exercise is aimed at restoring planned road networks and improving urban development within affected areas, particularly the GDP16 Layout in the Gombe State capital. […]
content:
The Gombe Geographic Information System (GOGIS) has commenced the allocation of alternative plots to individuals who unknowingly acquired lands illegally allocated on designated access roads in parts of Gombe metropolis.

The exercise is aimed at restoring planned road networks and improving urban development within affected areas, particularly the GDP16 Layout in the Gombe State capital.

Speaking to journalists during an inspection of the sites, the Director-General of GOGIS, Dr. Kabiru Usman Hassan, said the intervention followed a comprehensive land audit conducted three years ago.

According to him, the audit uncovered multiple land allocations, illegal developments, and distortions that disrupted the area’s road network and undermined proper urban planning.

  • NIPSS attack: FG pledges support for families of slain officers
  • Iran Foreign Ministry Counters Revolutionary Guards, Says Hormuz Remains Open

Dr. Hassan explained that some of the affected plots had been illegally allocated on designated access roads without the knowledge of the buyers.

He noted that the state government approved alternative plots for the affected beneficiaries to ensure they do not suffer losses due to circumstances beyond their control.

“The government recognised that many beneficiaries acquired the plots through official channels or third parties without knowing that they were not properly allocated. In line with the governor’s directive, alternative plots are being provided,” he said.

The GOGIS Director-General stated that the exercise is part of broader efforts to restore connectivity within the layout, improve traffic circulation, and create a more conducive environment for residents.

He further highlighted ongoing reforms in land administration aimed at promoting orderly urban development and eliminating irregularities in land allocation. He cited the Shongo Gardens Layout and the Shehu Abubakar District as examples of areas that have undergone significant modernization and planning reforms.

Dr. Hassan advised prospective land buyers to engage legal practitioners and conduct proper land verification through GOGIS before purchasing any property.

He emphasized that carrying out a legal search would help buyers confirm the authenticity and status of any land transaction and prevent future disputes.

One of the beneficiaries, Abubakar Ibrahim, described the reallocation of his plot as a major relief after years of uncertainty.

According to him, he unknowingly purchased a plot situated on an access road about seven years ago and had made several unsuccessful attempts to resolve the issue before the government’s intervention.

He commended the Gombe State Government for introducing reforms through GOGIS, noting that the agency’s digital land administration system has improved transparency, efficiency, and ease of land verification.

Ibrahim expressed optimism that the ongoing reforms would strengthen public confidence in land administration and contribute to the state’s orderly development
summary: The Gombe Geographic Information System (GOGIS) is allocating alternative plots to individuals who unknowingly acquired lands on designated access roads in Gombe metropolis, restoring planned road networks and improving urban development. This initiative indicates a growing focus on orderly urban development and land administration in Nigeria, which may positively impact the Lagos real estate market. The exercise is part of broader efforts to restore connectivity and improve traffic circulation, with 3 years of land audit and ongoing reforms.
trending_topics: land administration, urban development, orderly planning, Nigeria real estate

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: May positively impact Mixta's position in the Lagos market by demonstrating a growing focus on orderly urban development and land administration, which could lead to increased investor confidence and demand for Mixta's projects.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Potential opportunity to collaborate with government agencies or private developers to implement similar initiatives in Lagos, enhancing Mixta's reputation and market presence.

title: Firm Targets N250bn Capital Raise To Scale Real Estate
description: Firm Targets N250bn Capital Raise To Scale Real Estate  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: Haldane McCall Plc has unveiled plans for a N250 billion capital raise through fixed income securities to expand its real estate and hospitality footprint, subject to regulatory approvals. The announcement came as part of the resolutions during the company’s 2nd Annual General Meeting (AGM), where shareholders approved a dividend payout of N169.09 million, amounting to five kobo per share and expressed strong confidence in the Board and Management for what they described as an exemplary performance in a difficult macroeconomic environment marked by inflationary pressures and foreign exchange volatility. The company reported a profit after tax of N642.46 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, slightly below the N679.63 million recorded in the previous year. Revenue stood at N2.28 billion, with gross profit at N1.81 billion and operating profit of N754.9 million. Total assets rose to N21.83 billion, reflecting improved balance sheet strength and sustained business growth. Shareholders unanimously passed all the resolutions presented at the AGM, including the re-election of retiring directors, further signalling confidence in the Board’s leadership and long-term strategy. RELATED NEWS Investors Stake N1.8trn on Fixed Income Bills NDYC Seeks Clarification On Yari’s Alleged Acquisition Of Geregu Power NAFDAC Cracks Down On Smuggling, Seizes Vegetable Oil, N500m Soaps Chairman of the Board, Chief George Oguntade, reaffirmed the company’s disciplined approach to capital management, stating: “The Board remains committed to a disciplined capital allocation framework that balances immediate shareholder returns with long-term value creation. “Historically, dividend distributions have accounted for approximately 30 per cent of total shareholder returns, while the remaining 70 per cent has been reinvested to support growth, asset acquisition, and balance sheet expansion.” Speaking on the company’s momentum, Group managing director, Dr. Edward Akinlade, highlighted the expanding investor base and sustained confidence in the business, noting that the group’s shareholders grew from 464 in 2024 to 1,465 in 2026, reflecting strong market trust in its long-term direction. “The group’s consistent growth in shareholders’ equity over the past five years underscores the effectiveness of this approach and reflects management’s ability to execute strategy prudently in varying market conditions,” said Akinlade. A shareholder, Olatunji Bamidele, also hailed the Board and Management for their performance, saying: “The Board and Management have delivered an exemplary performance despite the challenging operating environment. The resilience and consistency demonstrated by the company are commendable.” Market watchers noted that the AGM underscored a year of steady financial performance, strengthened investor confidence, and a bold forward-looking capital strategy aimed at scaling Haldane McCall Plc’s operations and long-term value creation. We’ve got the edge. Get real
summary: Haldane McCall Plc plans a N250 billion capital raise through fixed income securities to expand its real estate and hospitality footprint, subject to regulatory approvals. This is a significant development for the Lagos market, indicating a substantial increase in investment in the real estate sector. The company's profit after tax of N642.46 million and revenue of N2.28 billion suggest a growing business.
trending_topics: real estate investment, capital raise, Lagos market

mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The growing demand for real estate in Lagos presents an opportunity for Mixta to expand its portfolio and explore new projects.

title: Stakeholders Seek Urgent Solutions as Housing Deficit Surpasses 20 Million Units
description: Stakeholders Seek Urgent Solutions as Housing Deficit Surpasses 20 Million Units  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: Housing Shortfall Surpasses 20 Million Homes in Nigeria housingnational news Jun 19 Written By Ayomide Fiyinfunoluwa Stakeholders Call for Urgent Action on Housing Shortfall Nigeria’s housing deficit has surpassed 20 million units, underscoring the scale of the country’s housing challenge and the urgent need for coordinated action to increase supply, improve affordability and expand access to housing finance. Industry stakeholders say the widening gap between housing demand and available supply continues to place pressure on households, particularly in major urban centres where population growth and rising construction costs are driving accommodation shortages. / You Might Also Like / The 2026 Dangote Refinery Blueprint: Location, IPO, and Global Energy Impact The Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway: Transforming Nigeria’s Infrastructure Landscape Nigeria’s Housing Deficit: How Big Is the Problem in 2026? Mistakes First-Time Property Investors Make in Nigeria The latest estimates reinforce concerns that current housing delivery levels remain insufficient to meet the needs of Nigeria’s rapidly growing population, which is projected to exceed 400 million people within the coming decades. Demand Continues to Outpace Supply Housing experts attribute the growing deficit to a combination of rapid urbanisation, population growth, inadequate financing and high construction costs. As more Nigerians migrate to urban centres in search of economic opportunities, demand for housing has increased significantly. However, the pace of residential development has not kept up with this growth, creating a persistent supply gap across both affordable and middle-income housing segments. Industry stakeholders note that millions of households continue to face challenges accessing quality and affordable accommodation, particularly in Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt, Kano and other major cities. Rising Construction Costs Worsen the Challenge The cost of delivering housing projects has increased substantially in recent years due to inflation, foreign exchange pressures and rising prices of building materials. Developers say the high cost of cement, steel, roofing materials and other construction inputs has affected project viability and slowed housing delivery across the country. These challenges have made it increasingly difficult for developers to produce homes at price points affordable to the average Nigerian household, further widening the gap between demand and supply. Limited Housing Finance Remains a Key Constraint Access to affordable mortgage financing remains one of the biggest obstacles to homeownership in Nigeria. Industry analysts point out that high interest rates, limited long-term funding and low mortgage penetration continue to restrict access to housing finance. As a result, many prospective homeowners are unable to secure the funding needed to purchase residential properties. Developers have also repeatedly highlighted difficulties accessing affordable project fi
summary: The article reports that Nigeria's housing deficit has surpassed 20 million units, with demand outpacing supply, particularly in major urban centers. This means that the Lagos market, among others, faces significant pressure due to rapid urbanization and rising construction costs. With over 400 million people projected to live in Nigeria within the coming decades, the demand for housing in Lagos is expected to increase.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Increased demand for Mixta's projects such as Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe due to the overall shortage of housing in Lagos
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Potential for Mixta to focus on affordable and mid-market housing segments to address the supply gap
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Rising construction costs could impact project profitability and delivery timelines for Mixta's developments

title: Judges’ Housing Celebration: ADC Blasts APC, Says It Undermines Judicial Independence
description: Judges’ Housing Celebration: ADC Blasts APC, Says It Undermines Judicial Independence  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) upraided the All Progressives Congress (APC) for celebrating the commissioning of residential quarters for judges. The opposition said it was a shameless, unethical and inappropriate spectacle that further eroded public confidence in the independence of the judiciary. It added that while judges deserve decent accommodation, adequate security and proper welfare, “these must not be packaged as personal achievement of government officials or as favours from politicians.” The ADC added that those provisions are constitutional obligations of the government funded by the Nigerian taxpayers. The party reacted to a statement by the APC commending President Bola Tinubu and FCT minister Nyesom Wike over the commissioning of the recently completed Court of Appeal Complex with state of the art amenities, and the 20 unit residential quarters for Court of Appeal justices and federal high court judges, and the upcoming commissioning of 20 unit residential quarters for the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) judges in Abuja. “The issue is therefore not the construction of the quarters in itself, but the dangerous impression of the Executive arm of government presenting itself as the benefactor of another constitutionally independent arm,” the National Publicity Secretary of ADC, Malam Bolaji Abdullahi said yesterday. He continued, “Neither President Bola Tinubu nor Minister Nyesom Wike is therefore entitled to personal acclaim for discharging responsibilities financed from public funds.” RELATED NEWS Delta SDP Chairman Commends INEC Over Restoration of Prof. Gombe INEC Has Finally Recognised Our Leadership – SDP INEC Rolls Out Ballot Materials For Nasarawa Senatorial By-election Abdullahi stressed that what the APC has done, by awarding personal acclaim to both the President and his minister for building houses for judges, is to create the impression that the welfare of judges is subject to the whims and caprice of government officials or to executive benevolence. He added that in every constitutional democracy governed by the principle of separation of powers, the Judiciary must never be placed in a position where its welfare can be publicly portrayed as a favour granted by political actors whose actions and interests may ultimately come before the courts. “What makes this development particularly troubling is the APC’s attempt to glorify President Tinubu and Minister Wike as patrons of the Judiciary. This action alone creates the unmistakable appearance that judicial welfare is dependent on executive benevolence rather than constitutional entitlement. It sends the wrong signal to Nigerians and inevitably raises legitimate concerns about the proper boundaries between the Executive and the Judiciary. This is particularly disturbing given the widespread perception that the President and this particular minister, Wike, already wield undue influence over the judiciary. The statement by the ruling party celebrating the duo can only
summary: The article reports on the African Democratic Congress (ADC) criticizing the All Progressives Congress (APC) for celebrating the commissioning of residential quarters for judges in Abuja, which the ADC sees as undermining judicial independence. This development has implications for the Lagos real estate market, particularly in the context of government-funded infrastructure projects. The article mentions 20 unit residential quarters for Court of Appeal justices and federal high court judges, and another 20 unit residential quarters for FCT judges.
trending_topics: judicial independence, government-funded infrastructure, Lagos real estate

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The article highlights the importance of government-funded infrastructure projects, which could influence Mixta's future projects in Lagos.

title: Resida launches all-in-one platform to simplify Nigeria’s rental process
description: Resida launches all-in-one platform to simplify Nigeria’s rental process  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Resida launches all-in-one platform to simplify Nigeria’s rental process By : Guardian Nigeria Date: 19 June 2026 4:33am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover lemobayo Kings Ayejuni is the founder of Resida. lemobayo Kings Ayejuni is the founder of Resida. Resida, a property technology startup focused on transforming the rental experience in Nigeria, has officially launched its all-in-one housing platform designed to simplify how Nigerians search for homes, pay rent and manage property-related services. The platform enters the market at a time when renters across major cities continue to face challenges ranging from fraudulent property listings and fragmented rental processes to poor tenancy record management and limited access to reliable maintenance services. Resida seeks to address these challenges by bringing multiple housing services into a single digital ecosystem. Through the platform, users can search verified and inspected properties, apply for both short-let and long-term rentals, make secure rent payments and access trusted artisans for repairs and maintenance. Speaking on the launch, Founder of Resida, Ilemobayo Kings Ayejuni, said the company was created to tackle longstanding inefficiencies within Nigeria’s rental market. “Renting in Lagos has never had the infrastructure it deserves. For too long, the rental experience has been stressful, fragmented and built on trust gaps. We built Resida because tenants, agents and landlords deserve a process that is transparent, secure and simple, from the first property search to rent payment and ongoing maintenance,” he said. Related News Sanwo-Olu, Nola break ground for Veritasi Homes, COOPLAG 22-floor Oyster Towers in Eko Atlantic City Minimalist & Co unveils M120 Apartments, setting new benchmark for lifestyle living in Lekki Eventhive sixth Lagos real estate fest to focus on innovation, investment Beyond serving renters, the platform also provides landlords and property managers with tools to improve operational efficiency. These include tenant verification services, automated rent collection, financial reporting features and maintenance request tracking systems. According to the company, Resida is currently active in more than 15 locations across Lagos, with a growing inventory of verified properties available to prospective tenants. The launch reflects the increasing role of technology in addressing challenges within Nigeria’s housing sector, as property technology firms continue to develop digital solutions aimed at improving housing access, transparency and property management. Available on Android, iOS and web platforms, the Resida app enables users to discover verified properties, submit rental applications, manage rent payments, maintain tenancy records and connect with trusted service providers, all from a single platform designed to make renting easier, safer and more efficient. Tags: House Rental Market Housing Sector Nigeria Lagos real estate Nigerian Rentals Property Managem
summary: Resida launches an all-in-one platform to simplify Nigeria's rental process, addressing challenges such as fraudulent listings and poor tenancy record management, which could improve the rental experience for approximately 20 million residents in Lagos. This development may increase transparency and security in the Lagos rental market. The platform's success could influence the market's demand for similar services.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Potential increase in demand for Mixta's properties in Lagos, such as Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe, due to improved rental experience
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Partnership or integration opportunity with Resida to enhance Mixta's property management services

title: 7-Fifteen Capital expands luxury real estate
description: 7-Fifteen Capital expands luxury real estate  Daily Trust
content: Luxury real estate development company 7-Fifteen Capital Limited has formally launched its Lagos office, marking a significant milestone in the firm’s expansion into Nigeria’s commercial capital and one of Africa’s most vibrant property markets. The high-profile launch ceremony, held in Ikoyi over the weekend, brought together leading business executives, investors, policymakers, and industry stakeholders, underscoring the growing interest in premium real estate developments within Lagos. The event commenced with an art exhibition titled Load-Bearing, curated by Yenwa Gallery and featuring five sculptural works commissioned by 7-Fifteen Capital. The exhibition provided guests with a visual exploration of the company’s architectural journey and development philosophy, showcasing interpretations of its flagship projects, including Zuri Residences on George Street, Ikoyi, which is currently under development. Among the prominent guests at the event were Chairman of Dangote Group, Aliko Dangote; Chairman of Metis Capital Partners, Hakeem Belo-Osagie; Founder of Stanbic IBTC and ANAP Foundation, Atedo Peterside; Co-Founder of Access Bank and Coronation Group, Aigboje Aig-Imoukhuede; Minister of Industry, Trade and Investment, Jumoke Oduwole; Minister of Education, Dr. Maruf Tunji Alausa; and Lagos State Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu. Delivering the keynote address, Governor Sanwo-Olu highlighted Lagos’ continued economic growth and its emergence as a global destination for real estate investment. He emphasized the role of quality developers in shaping the city’s future and formally welcomed 7-Fifteen Capital to Lagos. The governor expressed confidence in the company’s ability to contribute meaningfully to the state’s urban landscape through excellence in project delivery, innovative design, and sustainable development practices. Speaking at the event, Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder of 7-Fifteen Capital, Tope Adekoya, said the company’s expansion into Lagos represents a continuation of its commitment to creating refined residential communities. According to him, the vision that guided the development of projects such as Myari Lakeside, The Residence, and The Cove Lakeside in Abuja remains central to the company’s approach as it enters the Lagos market. “Now, as we enter Lagos, a city driven by energy, ambition and constant evolution, we do so with the same philosophy and a long-term commitment to contributing meaningful value to its future landscape,” Adekoya said. The launch also featured a panel discussion titled From Land to Keys: Fixing the Gaps in Lagos Real Estate Delivery, where industry experts examined challenges and opportunities within the state’s property sector, including project execution, financing, infrastructure, and regulatory frameworks. A major highlight of the evening was the unveiling of the 7-Fifteen Foundation, the company’s corporate social responsibility and social impact initiative aimed at improving access to opportun
summary: 7-Fifteen Capital, a luxury real estate development company, has launched its Lagos office, marking a significant milestone in the firm's expansion into Nigeria's commercial capital. This development underscores the growing interest in premium real estate developments within Lagos, with Governor Sanwo-Olu expressing confidence in the company's ability to contribute meaningfully to the state's urban landscape. The event highlighted Lagos' continued economic growth and emergence as a global destination for real estate investment.
trending_topics: luxury real estate, Lagos property market, premium developments

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The growing demand for luxury real estate in Lagos may create a competitive market for Mixta's premium offerings, such as Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe.

title: Wealth Preservation Drives Rising Real Estate Investments Among Africa’s Elite
description: Wealth Preservation Drives Rising Real Estate Investments Among Africa’s Elite  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: Africa’s Ultra-Wealthy Increase Real Estate Investments Amid Global Economic Uncertainty housingnational news Jun 8 Written By Ayomide Fiyinfunoluwa Affluent African Investors Increase Exposure to Property Markets Africa’s ultra-high-net-worth individuals (UHNWIs) are increasing their exposure to real estate as global economic uncertainty, market volatility and inflationary pressures reshape investment strategies across the continent. According to a recent report by Standard Bank Group, affluent investors with more than $50 million in investable assets are increasingly turning to residential and commercial property to preserve wealth, generate income and diversify portfolios. / You Might Also Like / The 2026 Dangote Refinery Blueprint: Location, IPO, and Global Energy Impact The Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway: Transforming Nigeria’s Infrastructure Landscape Nigeria’s Housing Deficit: How Big Is the Problem in 2026? Mistakes First-Time Property Investors Make in Nigeria The trend reflects a broader shift toward tangible assets as wealthy investors seek protection against currency fluctuations, geopolitical risks and unpredictable financial markets. Property investments are increasingly viewed as a stable asset class capable of delivering both long-term capital appreciation and recurring income through rental yields. Property Emerges as a Safe-Haven Asset According to Standard Bank Group, demand for real estate among the bank’s wealthiest clients has accelerated significantly in recent years. The institution reported that acquisitions of residential and commercial properties by ultra-wealthy clients in South Africa more than doubled in the 12 months leading to September 2025 compared with the previous year. Industry analysts attribute the trend to growing concerns over inflation, currency depreciation and volatility in traditional asset classes. Unlike financial instruments that can be affected by rapid market swings, property assets offer investors a tangible store of value with the potential to deliver steady income and long-term growth. Chris Browne, Group Head of Wealth and Investment at Standard Bank Group, noted that wealthy investors increasingly favour assets capable of preserving value while providing sustainable returns over time. Residential and Commercial Assets Attract Capital The report indicates that both residential and commercial property segments are benefiting from increased investor interest. Residential real estate remains attractive for wealth preservation, lifestyle considerations and long-term appreciation, while commercial assets continue to appeal to investors seeking predictable cash flows and inflation-linked returns. Premium office developments, mixed-use projects, logistics facilities and high-end residential properties are among the asset classes attracting significant capital from wealthy investors across the continent. The growing interest in logistics and industrial assets also reflects the expansion of regional tra
summary: The article reports that Africa's ultra-high-net-worth individuals are increasing their exposure to real estate, with acquisitions of residential and commercial properties in South Africa more than doubling in the 12 months leading to September 2025. This trend is likely to have a positive impact on the Lagos real estate market, as wealthy investors seek stable assets. With over $50 million in investable assets, these investors are expected to drive demand for high-end properties.
trending_topics: wealth preservation, real estate investments, ultra-high-net-worth individuals

title: Nigerian Journalist Launches Global Real Estate News Platform
description: Nigerian Journalist Launches Global Real Estate News Platform  Techpoint Africa
content: Nigerian realtor and journalist Alefia Friday James, based in Abuja, has launched EstateWire247, a global real estate news platform dedicated to providing comprehensive coverage of property markets, investment trends, and housing developments across the world. The platform, which officially commenced operations in May 2026, is positioned as a 24-hour digital publication delivering news, analysis, and insights on residential and commercial real estate across Africa, Europe, Asia, the Middle East, and the Americas. Speaking on the launch, James said the initiative was established to address the growing demand for reliable, structured, and investor-focused information within the global property industry. According to him, the platform will prioritize accurate, data-driven, and investor-oriented reporting, covering international property market developments, cross-border investments, urban growth trends, and emerging opportunities in both developed and emerging economies. The publication will feature daily news updates, in-depth market analysis, exclusive interviews with industry experts, and opinion articles focusing on real estate finance, housing policy, construction trends, and property technology innovations shaping the future of the sector. He noted that the global real estate industry continues to evolve rapidly, driven by urbanization, population growth, infrastructure expansion, and increasing investor interest in property as a long-term asset class. EstateWire247 is expected to serve a broad audience that includes investors, developers, real estate professionals, policymakers, researchers, and members of the diaspora seeking timely insights into global property market dynamics. James added that the platform reflects his commitment to advancing both journalism and real estate by bridging information gaps and providing credible, accessible coverage of the international property ecosystem.
summary: Alefia Friday James, a Nigerian realtor and journalist, has launched EstateWire247, a global real estate news platform providing comprehensive coverage of property markets, investment trends, and housing developments across the world. This platform is expected to serve a broad audience, including investors, developers, and policymakers, with daily news updates, in-depth market analysis, and opinion articles. For the Lagos market, this means increased global visibility and potential investment opportunities, especially considering the article's focus on emerging economies.
trending_topics: global real estate news, property markets, investment trends, housing developments

title: Africans with over $50 million are buying more property to protect their fortunes
description: Africans with over $50 million are buying more property to protect their fortunes  Business Insider Africa
content: Africa’s wealthiest individuals are increasingly turning to real estate as a haven for their fortunes, using property to preserve wealth, diversify investments, and generate long-term income amid growing economic and geopolitical uncertainty. Africans with over $50 million are buying more property to protect their fortunes Africa's wealthiest individuals are increasingly investing in real estate to preserve and diversify their wealth. South Africa has seen a doubling in residential and commercial property acquisitions by ultra-wealthy clients within a year, driven by lower interest rates and strong demand. Cape Town has emerged as Africa's wealth capital, attracting affluent local and international migrants due to its lifestyle and property market. Investors in countries like Kenya and Ghana view property as a hedge against inflation and currency instability, focusing on premium office, industrial, and logistics assets. ADVERTISEMENT Recommended articles markets US edges out China in Kenya with preliminary deal to control $62.4 billion untapped rare earth deposits markets South Africa’s diamond industry may face disruption as London-listed miner moves to restructure assets markets Nigeria advances $400 million rare earth project in push to move beyond raw mineral exports by Taboolaby Taboola Sponsored LinksSponsored Links Promoted LinksPromoted Links You May Like This Is Why Every American Should Consider Using This Retirement Loophole in 2026Protecting My Savings Learn More Temu Clearance SaleTemu Clearance Sale, where unbeatable discounts meet your shopping needsTEMU Shop Now According to new insights from Standard Bank, Africa’s largest lender by assets, high-net-worth individuals with at least $50 million in investable assets have significantly increased their exposure to residential, commercial, and industrial property across key African markets. The trend has been particularly pronounced in South Africa, where the number of residential and commercial properties acquired by the bank’s ultra-wealthy clients more than doubled in the 12 months to September 2025 compared with the previous year, Bloomberg reported. The buying spree accelerated after the South African Reserve Bank began cutting interest rates in 2024, making property financing more attractive. Yet, according to Standard Bank, investor appetite has remained resilient even as global uncertainties—including Middle East tensions, inflation concerns, and market volatility—continue to unsettle financial markets. ADVERTISEMENT The trend highlights a defining characteristic of Africa’s wealthy: a preference for long-term wealth preservation over short-term market speculation. Rather than reacting to political developments or temporary economic shocks, many affluent investors are positioning assets for future generations. Africans with over $50 million are buying more property to protect their fortunes BI Africa Millionaire Hubs Drive Demand Africa's wealth landscape is also expanding. Acc
summary: The article reports that Africans with over $50 million are increasingly investing in real estate to preserve and diversify their wealth, with a doubling of residential and commercial property acquisitions in South Africa within a year. This trend may indicate a growing demand for luxury properties in African markets, potentially benefiting high-end developments in Lagos. However, the article does not provide specific data on the Lagos market.
trending_topics: wealth management, real estate investment, African luxury market

summary: The article reports that the House of Representatives has flagged off a probe into the Real Estate Investment Fund, but the content is limited to just the headline. This could imply increased scrutiny and regulation in the real estate sector, potentially affecting the market's confidence in investments. For Lagos real estate, this could lead to a more cautious approach to investment, which might impact projects like Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe.
